forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,62 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: c380
+title: Remove no-provider/no-layer fallback from <FocusZone> and <FocusScope>
+---
+## What
+
+Delete the `FallbackFocusZoneBody` (kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx, lines ~530–648) and `FallbackFocusScopeBody` (kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx, lines ~449–558) branches and the conditional rendering that selects them. These exist solely so a `<FocusZone>` or `<FocusScope>` mounted outside the spatial provider stack (no `<SpatialFocusProvider>`, no `<FocusLayer>`) silently degrades to a plain `<div>` instead of throwing.
+
+The focus system exists to work. Mounting a primitive without its providers is a setup bug, not a supported mode. The fallback hides those bugs by treating them as no-error and is rationalized only by tests that skip the provider stack — production code never enters this branch (per the existing comments).
+
+### Files to modify
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx`
+  - Delete `FallbackFocusZoneBody`, `FallbackFocusZoneBodyProps`, and the no-spatial-context section header.
+  - In `FocusZone`, replace `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (line 226) with `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (which throws). Drop the `parentFq === null` branch in the `fq` `useMemo` and the `focusKey = fq ?? segment` fallback. The composed FQM is now non-null by construction.
+  - Delete the `hasSpatialContext` ternary in the JSX (~lines 263, 268–291) — render `<SpatialFocusZoneBody>` unconditionally.
+  - Remove the now-unused `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` import.
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`
+  - Delete `FallbackFocusScopeBody`, `FallbackFocusScopeBodyProps`, and the no-spatial-context section header.
+  - In `FocusScope`, replace `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (line 197) with `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()`. Drop the `parentFq === null` branch and the `focusKey = fq ?? segment` fallback.
+  - Delete the `hasSpatialContext` ternary (~lines 226, 233–256) — render `<SpatialFocusScopeBody>` unconditionally (the `!renderContainer` branch stays).
+  - Remove the now-unused `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` import.
+
+Keep `useOptionalFocusActions` / `useOptionalIsDirectFocus` usage inside the spatial bodies — those handle the separate (orthogonal) case of a missing `EntityFocusProvider`, which is not what this task targets. If the audit shows those hooks are no longer reachable from any caller after this change, delete them too; otherwise leave them.
+
+### Tests to update
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.test.tsx`
+  - Replace the `"renders a fallback div when mounted outside any FocusLayer (no spatial registration)"` test (line 255) with a regression test that asserts mounting `<FocusZone>` without a `<FocusLayer>` ancestor throws (assert via `expect(() => render(...)).toThrow(/FocusLayer/)`).
+
+- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx`
+  - Replace the `"falls back to a plain div when no <FocusLayer> ancestor is mounted"` test (line 1048) with the same throw-asserting regression test, scoped to `<FocusScope>`.
+  - The bare-render tests at lines 621 (`registers/unregisters scope in entity-focus registry`) and 867 (the `useIsDirectFocus` selective-wake counter) currently mount `<FocusScope>` inside only `<EntityFocusProvider>`. Wrap them with `<SpatialFocusProvider>` + `<FocusLayer name={asSegment("window")}>` so the primitive's required ancestors are present. Use the existing `renderWithFocus` helper (focus-scope.test.tsx:137) where it fits.
+  - Update the surrounding describe-block doc comment (focus-scope.test.tsx:906–916) to remove the "bulk of this file exercise the no-spatial-context fallback path" claim, which is no longer accurate after this task.
+
+### Audit step (do this before deleting)
+
+Grep across `kanban-app/ui/src` for `<FocusZone` and `<FocusScope` in test files. For each test that does not have `<SpatialFocusProvider>` + `<FocusLayer>` as ancestors of the primitive, either wrap it or delete it (deleting is correct when the test exists only to prove the fallback works). Production callers must already be inside a `<FocusLayer>` per current architecture; verify by greps but no production source change is expected.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `FallbackFocusZoneBody` and `FallbackFocusScopeBody` no longer exist in the source tree (`grep -r FallbackFocus` returns no matches outside this task description and history).
+- [ ] `FocusZone` and `FocusScope` use `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (the throwing variant), not `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()`.
+- [ ] Mounting `<FocusZone moniker={...} />` without an enclosing `<FocusLayer>` throws a clear error naming `FocusLayer` (and same for `<FocusScope>`).
+- [ ] All previously bare-mounted tests in `focus-scope.test.tsx` and `focus-zone.test.tsx` are either wrapped in the spatial provider stack or deleted; no test asserts the no-spatial-context plain-div behavior.
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` (unit + browser) passes with zero skipped tests added by this change.
+- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` passes with no `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` references in `focus-zone.tsx` / `focus-scope.tsx`.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] Add `it("throws when <FocusZone> is mounted without <FocusLayer>", () => { expect(() => render(<FocusZone moniker={asSegment("ui:orphan")}>{null}</FocusZone>)).toThrow(/FocusLayer/); });` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.test.tsx`.
+- [ ] Add the parallel `it("throws when <FocusScope> is mounted without <FocusLayer>", ...)` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx`.
+- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test -- focus-zone focus-scope` and confirm the new throw-tests pass and no fallback test remains.
+- [ ] Run the full suite `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` to catch any other test that was relying on the silent-fallback path.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the throw-asserting tests first (RED), then delete the fallback bodies and switch to the throwing FQM hook (GREEN), then audit and fix any other tests that break (still GREEN).

changes.value:
## What

Delete the `FallbackFocusZoneBody` (kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx, lines ~530–648) and `FallbackFocusScopeBody` (kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx, lines ~449–558) branches and the conditional rendering that selects them. These exist solely so a `<FocusZone>` or `<FocusScope>` mounted outside the spatial provider stack (no `<SpatialFocusProvider>`, no `<FocusLayer>`) silently degrades to a plain `<div>` instead of throwing.

The focus system exists to work. Mounting a primitive without its providers is a setup bug, not a supported mode. The fallback hides those bugs by treating them as no-error and is rationalized only by tests that skip the provider stack — production code never enters this branch (per the existing comments).

### Files to modify

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.tsx`
  - Delete `FallbackFocusZoneBody`, `FallbackFocusZoneBodyProps`, and the no-spatial-context section header.
  - In `FocusZone`, replace `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (line 226) with `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (which throws). Drop the `parentFq === null` branch in the `fq` `useMemo` and the `focusKey = fq ?? segment` fallback. The composed FQM is now non-null by construction.
  - Delete the `hasSpatialContext` ternary in the JSX (~lines 263, 268–291) — render `<SpatialFocusZoneBody>` unconditionally.
  - Remove the now-unused `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` import.

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.tsx`
  - Delete `FallbackFocusScopeBody`, `FallbackFocusScopeBodyProps`, and the no-spatial-context section header.
  - In `FocusScope`, replace `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (line 197) with `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()`. Drop the `parentFq === null` branch and the `focusKey = fq ?? segment` fallback.
  - Delete the `hasSpatialContext` ternary (~lines 226, 233–256) — render `<SpatialFocusScopeBody>` unconditionally (the `!renderContainer` branch stays).
  - Remove the now-unused `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` import.

Keep `useOptionalFocusActions` / `useOptionalIsDirectFocus` usage inside the spatial bodies — those handle the separate (orthogonal) case of a missing `EntityFocusProvider`, which is not what this task targets. If the audit shows those hooks are no longer reachable from any caller after this change, delete them too; otherwise leave them.

### Tests to update

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.test.tsx`
  - Replace the `"renders a fallback div when mounted outside any FocusLayer (no spatial registration)"` test (line 255) with a regression test that asserts mounting `<FocusZone>` without a `<FocusLayer>` ancestor throws (assert via `expect(() => render(...)).toThrow(/FocusLayer/)`).

- `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx`
  - Replace the `"falls back to a plain div when no <FocusLayer> ancestor is mounted"` test (line 1048) with the same throw-asserting regression test, scoped to `<FocusScope>`.
  - The bare-render tests at lines 621 (`registers/unregisters scope in entity-focus registry`) and 867 (the `useIsDirectFocus` selective-wake counter) currently mount `<FocusScope>` inside only `<EntityFocusProvider>`. Wrap them with `<SpatialFocusProvider>` + `<FocusLayer name={asSegment("window")}>` so the primitive's required ancestors are present. Use the existing `renderWithFocus` helper (focus-scope.test.tsx:137) where it fits.
  - Update the surrounding describe-block doc comment (focus-scope.test.tsx:906–916) to remove the "bulk of this file exercise the no-spatial-context fallback path" claim, which is no longer accurate after this task.

### Audit step (do this before deleting)

Grep across `kanban-app/ui/src` for `<FocusZone` and `<FocusScope` in test files. For each test that does not have `<SpatialFocusProvider>` + `<FocusLayer>` as ancestors of the primitive, either wrap it or delete it (deleting is correct when the test exists only to prove the fallback works). Production callers must already be inside a `<FocusLayer>` per current architecture; verify by greps but no production source change is expected.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `FallbackFocusZoneBody` and `FallbackFocusScopeBody` no longer exist in the source tree (`grep -r FallbackFocus` returns no matches outside this task description and history).
- [ ] `FocusZone` and `FocusScope` use `useFullyQualifiedMoniker()` (the throwing variant), not `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker()`.
- [ ] Mounting `<FocusZone moniker={...} />` without an enclosing `<FocusLayer>` throws a clear error naming `FocusLayer` (and same for `<FocusScope>`).
- [ ] All previously bare-mounted tests in `focus-scope.test.tsx` and `focus-zone.test.tsx` are either wrapped in the spatial provider stack or deleted; no test asserts the no-spatial-context plain-div behavior.
- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` (unit + browser) passes with zero skipped tests added by this change.
- [ ] `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui typecheck` passes with no `useOptionalFullyQualifiedMoniker` references in `focus-zone.tsx` / `focus-scope.tsx`.

## Tests

- [ ] Add `it("throws when <FocusZone> is mounted without <FocusLayer>", () => { expect(() => render(<FocusZone moniker={asSegment("ui:orphan")}>{null}</FocusZone>)).toThrow(/FocusLayer/); });` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-zone.test.tsx`.
- [ ] Add the parallel `it("throws when <FocusScope> is mounted without <FocusLayer>", ...)` in `kanban-app/ui/src/components/focus-scope.test.tsx`.
- [ ] Run `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test -- focus-zone focus-scope` and confirm the new throw-tests pass and no fallback test remains.
- [ ] Run the full suite `pnpm -C kanban-app/ui test` to catch any other test that was relying on the silent-fallback path.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the throw-asserting tests first (RED), then delete the fallback bodies and switch to the throwing FQM hook (GREEN), then audit and fix any other tests that break (still GREEN).
